items.title: What is IBM Business Automation?
items.text:
In a world driven by processes, chaos is the silent killer of efficiency…

Business Automation technology is designed to tackle this chaos head on.

With technology applied in both front and back offices to: - Perform repetitive tasks - Make adaptive decisions - Manage enterprise data - Optimize processes - With minimal human intervention

items.title: Headers
items.text:
# H1 Header
## H2 Header
### H3 Header
#### H4 Header
##### H5 Header
###### H6 Header

items.title: Links and images
items.text:
[Link text](https://example.com)
[Link with title](https://example.com "Hover title")
![Alt text](image.jpg)
![Image with title](image.jpg "Image title")

items.title: Lists
items.text:
Unordered:
- Item 1
- Item 2
  - Nested item

Ordered:
1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item

items.title: Blockquotes
items.text:
> This is a blockquote
> Multiple lines
>> Nested quote

items.title: Tables
items.text:
| Header 1 | Header 2 | Header 3 |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Row 1    | Data     | Data     |
| Row 2    | Data     | Data     |

items.title: Task lists
items.text:
- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Incomplete task
- [ ] Another task

items.title: Line breaks
items.text:
End a line with two spaces  
to create a line break.

Or use a blank line for a new paragraph.

items.title: Pick Your Path
items.text:
Not sure where to start? Pick the path that matches what you're trying to do:

I want to... Start here Parse HTML I already have Querying elements — CSS, XPath, and text-based selection Quickly scrape a page and prototype Pick a fetcher and test right away, or launch the interactive shell Build a crawler that scales Spiders — concurrent, multi-session crawls with pause/resume Scrape without writing code CLI extract commands or hook up the MCP server to your favourite AI tool Migrate from another library From BeautifulSoup or Scrapy comparison

Again, this is just the tip of the iceberg with this fetcher. Check out the rest from here for all details and the complete list of arguments.

That's Scrapling at a glance. If you want to learn more, continue to the next section.
